sections.heading_path: Confined Space Entry Procedure > 8. Confined Space Entry > 8.8
sections.hp_original_text:
**8.8.** Entry Supervisor (Local Area Authority) will ensure that:

- a. A pre-entry meeting is conducted to ensure H&P and 3rd-party contractor employees involved in entry operations are aware of the hazards that may be faced during entry, the warning signs of symptoms, the consequences of exposure, emergency response and evacuation procedures.

- b. The Confined Space Entry Permit form has been completed after all actions and conditions necessary for safe entry including blocking, locking, and tagging have been performed and permit requirements have been communicated to all personnel involved.
  - i. The Confined Space Entry Permit contains the required information and that the necessary procedures, practices and equipment are being utilized.
  - ii. The Confined Space Entry Permit is posted at the entryway to the space or otherwise made available so that entrants can assure themselves that entry preparations have been satisfactorily completed.

- c. All confined spaces are monitored continuously to confirm safe conditions exist; and if not, entrants are evacuated, and the permit is cancelled.
- d. Appropriate measures are taken to prevent entry of unauthorized personnel and provide the means to remove them from all confined spaces. Rescue services for Confined Space entry and a means for summoning them are readily available in an emergency. Phone numbers for 3rd-party emergency rescue services (local fire station) will be posted near a phone capable of calling outside location.

merge_result.merged_text:
**8.8.** The Entry Supervisor (Local Area Authority), and where regional procedures assign equivalent duties to a Person in Charge (PIC), will ensure that:

- a. A pre-entry meeting is conducted to ensure H&P and 3rd-party contractor employees involved in entry operations are aware of the hazards that may be faced during entry, the warning signs and symptoms, the consequences of exposure, and the emergency response and evacuation procedures. In Oman operations, this pre-entry meeting shall be conducted as a Toolbox Talk (TRIC) **at the job site** by the person in charge and shall include all personnel involved in the planned task. Crew names shall be entered onto the TRIC card to establish accountability for who is going to do what, and every member of the work party shall sign the toolbox talk/TBT to confirm that the hazards, controls, and ERP are understood. If the job changes or a new person joins the team, a Time Out for Safety (TOFS) shall be taken; the new person shall be briefed on all identified hazards, the corresponding control measures, and any responsibilities expected of him, and the TRIC card shall be reviewed and amended to control any risk associated with the change.

- b. The Confined Space Entry Permit form has been completed after all actions and conditions necessary for safe entry, including blocking, locking, and tagging, have been performed, and permit requirements have been communicated to all personnel involved.
  - i. The Confined Space Entry Permit shall contain the required information and confirm that the necessary procedures, practices, and equipment are being utilized. In Oman operations, all other options must be considered before CSE is implemented. The PIC shall ensure that the entrant(s) and the standby man are trained and competent to perform the task and fully understand their roles and responsibilities, that the necessary documents including PTW, work instructions, JSA/TRA, checklists, and TRIC have been completed and are in place prior to authorising work to commence, and that the area supervisor is informed of the planned confined-space work to prevent SIMOPS or other conflicts of work. In Oman operations, the PIC shall also define the controls and measures to be observed throughout the task, including the frequency of air-quality sampling and gas testing of the atmosphere inside the space. In land drilling operations, the permit shall also capture the applicable acceptances and approvals, including Applicant / Person in Charge of Worksite and Toolpusher signatures; acceptance by the Person in charge of work stating, "I understand the requirements of this permit and consider it safe to perform the work," with signature and date/time; approval by the PIC (TP or NTP) stating, "I have personally checked the worksite and precautions and authorise this work to commence," with person in charge, signature, and date/time; and approval by the Client DSV onshift, if required, stating, "I have reviewed this permit and authorise the work described above to commence," with client DSV name, signature, and date/time.
  - ii. The Confined Space Entry Permit is posted at the entryway to the space or otherwise made available so that entrants can assure themselves that entry preparations have been satisfactorily completed.

- c. All confined spaces are monitored continuously to confirm safe conditions exist. Where regional procedures require additional atmospheric control detail, the defined frequency of air-quality sampling and gas testing shall be followed in addition to continuous monitoring; if safe conditions do not exist, entrants shall be evacuated and the permit shall be cancelled.

- d. Appropriate measures are taken to prevent entry of unauthorized personnel and provide the means to remove them from all confined spaces. Rescue services for confined-space entry, and a means for summoning them, are readily available in an emergency. Phone numbers for 3rd-party emergency rescue services (local fire station) will be posted near a phone capable of calling outside location. In Oman operations, the stand-by watchman shall maintain entry control using an Entry Board & Marker (See Appendix 5.2), test communications, test the gas monitor, check harnesses, safety lines, and stretcher, check the tripod/rescue device for operation, function-test emergency equipment, ensure there are no loose items around the opening, and verify the availability of gas monitors or air-quality sampling equipment, radios (if applicable), BA sets, resuscitator, and safety barrier. The standby man shall also maintain a personnel log showing NAME, TIME IN, and TIME OUT. Before any confined-space work commences in Oman operations, the medic must be briefed on the planned activity, prepare all necessary medical equipment, and, in the event of a call out (ERP), deploy to the worksite to ensure immediate access to the injured person or persons for medical assessment and treatment as required. In Saudi Arabia rig operations, a specific rescue plan shall be developed for confined-space scenarios on the rig. The rescue teams shall be oriented and made aware of the rescue procedures through training and safety drills and exercises, and rescue team members shall be informed of the entry and be readily available. In those Saudi Arabia operations, the confined-space rescue team composition shall include the Toolpusher as team leader, with the Site Medic, Assistant Driller, and Derrickman/Roustabout as team members.

sections.heading_path: Confined Space Entry Procedure > 9. Confined Space Exit
sections.hp_original_text:
## 9. Confined Space Exit

The exit from Confined Space and the returning of the area back to service will follow a systematic approach. The Authorized Attendant confirms that all personnel have exited the Confined Space and Entry Supervisor (Local Area Authority) notes any problems and closes Entry Permit. All hole covers and/or hatches will be re-installed immediately after completing the task.

sections.hp_original_text:
## 10. Training

H&P will provide training so that employees who work in Confined Spaces as Authorized Entrants, outside as Authorized Attendants or as Entry Supervisors (Local Area Authority), acquire the understanding, knowledge, and skills necessary to prevent injury or loss of life.

merge_result.merged_text:

sections.heading_path: Confined Space Entry Procedure > 10. Training > 10.1-10.3
sections.hp_original_text:
**10.1.** Training will be provided to each affected employee:

- a. Before the employee is involved in a Confined Space Entry
- b. Before there is a change in assigned duties
- c. Whenever there is a change in Confined Space Operations that presents a hazard about which the employees have not been previously trained
- d. Refresher training will be conducted annually and whenever the employer has reason to believe that there are either deviations from the Confined Space Entry Procedure or that there are inadequacies in the employee’s knowledge or use of these procedures.

**10.2.** Training will establish employee proficiency in the duties as authorized entrant, attendant, or entry supervisor (Local Area Authority). H&P will introduce new or revised procedures as necessary.

**10.3.** H&P will document that training as been completed.

merge_result.merged_text:
**10.1.** Training shall be provided to each affected employee:

- a. Before the employee is involved in a Confined Space Entry
- b. Before there is a change in assigned duties
- c. Whenever there is a change in Confined Space Operations that presents a hazard about which the employees have not been previously trained
- d. Refresher training shall be conducted annually and whenever the employer has reason to believe that there are either deviations from the Confined Space Entry Procedure or inadequacies in the employee’s knowledge or use of these procedures.

This training shall apply to all personnel involved in confined space activities, including planning, supervision, entry, atmospheric testing and monitoring, standby, or rescue, and to any personnel who in the course of their normal duties are expected to enter a confined space. As applicable to the operation, trained personnel shall include the PIC, permit requestor, entrant, risk assessment team, standby man, emergency response team, authorized gas tester, attendant, entry supervisor (Local Area Authority), Work Permit issuer, and Workgroup Leader. In Saudi Arabia operations, Work Permit issuers (the Toolpusher or delegated person) and Workgroup Leaders involved in confined space entry shall undergo Confined Space Entry Inspection Training.

**10.2.** Training shall establish employee proficiency in the duties as authorized entrant, attendant, or entry supervisor (Local Area Authority). H&P shall introduce new or revised procedures as necessary. Training shall also establish awareness of confined space hazards and control measures for all personnel involved in planning, supervision, entry, or rescue. Training shall cover the hazards of confined space entry and the use of required equipment, including SCBA where it must be utilized, and shall include formal practical demonstration. Personnel authorized to perform atmospheric testing and monitoring shall be instructed in the use of the monitoring equipment and shall undergo Gas Tester training. In Saudi Arabia operations, this equipment instruction is provided by the Safety Officer in accordance with HSE Training Procedure K-KS-HS-PR-007. Responsible persons and Emergency Response Teams shall be trained and exercised in the proper use of the installation- or facility-specific confined space rescue and retrieval equipment.

**10.3.** H&P shall document that training has been completed. Documentation shall include, as applicable, the formal practical training and demonstration on confined-space hazards and equipment use, including SCBA, the training of personnel authorized to conduct atmospheric testing and monitoring, and the training and drills for responsible persons and Emergency Response Teams on installation- or facility-specific confined space rescue and retrieval equipment. In Oman operations, this training and practical demonstration shall be given in a formal manner and fully documented, and the rescue and retrieval equipment training and drills shall also be documented.

sections.heading_path: Confined Space Entry Procedure > 11. 3rd-party Contractor Requirements
sections.hp_original_text:
## 11. 3rd-party Contractor Requirements

When a 3rd-party contractor will be performing work in an H&P owned Confined Space, the following steps will be completed by H&Ps Entry Supervisor (Local Area Authority).

merge_result.merged_text:
## 11. 3rd-party Contractor Requirements

When a 3rd-party contractor will be performing work in an H&P owned Confined Space, the following steps will be completed by H&Ps Entry Supervisor (Local Area Authority).

sections.heading_path: Confined Space Entry Procedure > 11. 3rd-party Contractor Requirements > 11.1-11.7
sections.hp_original_text:
**11.1.** Inform 3rd-party contractors that the work being performed will be conducted in a confined space.

**11.2.** Inform 3rd-party contractors why the space is designated as a confined space.

**11.3.** Inform 3rd-party contractors of past experiences and hazards associated with any confined spaces.

**11.4.** Inform 3rd-party contractors of any precautions, procedures and emergency response plans that have been implemented to protect all persons in the confined space.

**11.5.** If both H&P and Operator’s 3rd-party employees will be making entries, the entries will be coordinated between the H&P entry supervisor (Local Area Authority) and the Operator’s supervisor.

**11.6.** H&P’s entry supervisor (Local Area Authority) will confirm that confined space hazards and permit procedure training has been provided by the 3rd-party employer called out by H&P.

**11.7.** H&Ps entry supervisor (Local Area Authority) will debrief the 3rd-party employee(s) at the conclusion of the entry regarding the procedure used for entry and any hazards encountered or created in the Confined Space. Hazards will be noted on the Permit for future reference.
